9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.

11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID            string    `json:"id"`
14		Title         string    `json:"title"`
15		Status        string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority      int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType     string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		SchemaVersion int       `json:"schema_version"`

20		CreatedAt     time.Time `json:"created_at"`
21		CreatedBy     string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
22		UpdatedAt     time.Time `json:"updated_at"`

31		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format

66		// Dependency waits are derived from dependency status and do not persist as

70		// It is outside worker dispatch until transitioned to open or cancelled.

79	// TD-031 §2 and the JSON Schema enum at
80	// cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json. The CI guard tests in
81	// sync_test.go assert that schema, TD doc, and Go source agree with this
82	// list; adding or removing a value here without updating the schema and TD
83	// will fail CI.
84	var CanonicalStatuses = []string{

86		StatusInProgress,
87		StatusClosed,
88		StatusBlocked,
89		StatusProposed,
90		StatusCancelled,

93	// IsCanonicalStatus reports whether s is one of the persisted bead statuses
94	// in CanonicalStatuses.
95	func IsCanonicalStatus(s string) bool {

99			}

104	// IssueType constants
105	const (
106		// IssueTypeOperatorPrompt is the bead type used for operator-submitted
107		// prompts that the work runs as instructions.
108		IssueTypeOperatorPrompt = "operator-prompt"
109	)
110	
111	// Default labels and other defaults for operator-prompt beads.
112	const (
113		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
114		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
115		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
116		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
117		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
118		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
119		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
120	)
121	
122	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
123	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
124	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
125	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
126	//
127	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
128	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
129	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
130	//
131	// Allow/deny matrix:
132	//
133	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
134	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
135	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
136	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
137	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
138		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
139			return nil
140		}
141		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
142			return nil
143		}
144		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
145	}
146	
147	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
148	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
149	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
150	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
151	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
152	// caller-supplied priority (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
153	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
154	//
155	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
156	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
157	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
158		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
159		title := body
160		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
161			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
162		}
163		if title == "" {
164			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
165		}
166		priority := defaultTier
167		if priority < MinPriority {
168			priority = MinPriority
169		}
170		if priority > MaxPriority {
171			priority = MaxPriority
172		}
173		return &Bead{
174			Title:       title,
175			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
176			Status:      StatusProposed,
177			Priority:    priority,
178			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
179			Description: body,
180			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
181		}
182	}

187		DefaultStatus        = StatusOpen
188		DefaultPriority      = 2
189		DefaultPrefix        = "bx" // used only when repo name detection fails
190		CurrentSchemaVersion = 1
191		MinPriority          = 0
192		MaxPriority          = 4
193	)

243	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
244	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
245		var ids []string
246		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
247			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
248		}
249		return ids
250	}
251	
252	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
253	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
254		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
255			if d.DependsOnID == id {
256				return true
257			}
258		}
259		return false
260	}
261	
262	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
263	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
264		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
265			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
266				return // already exists
267			}
268		}
269		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
270			IssueID:     b.ID,
271			DependsOnID: depID,
272			Type:        depType,
273			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
274		})
275	}
276	
277	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
278	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
279		var filtered []Dependency
280		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
281			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
282				filtered = append(filtered, d)
283			}
284		}
285		b.Dependencies = filtered
286	}

1	// Package executeloop defines the canonical spec type for work workers.
2	// It is import-safe: no dependency on cobra, server, or transport packages.
3	package executeloop
4	
5	import (
6		"encoding/json"
7		"fmt"
8		"strconv"
9		"time"
10	
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/escalation"
12	)
13	
14	// SpecCurrentVersion is the single spec version supported by this client and
15	// server. Forward-compat stance: a client MUST set SpecVersion = SpecCurrentVersion;
16	// a server receiving an unknown version SHOULD reject with an error rather than
17	// silently misinterpreting fields. There is no backwards-compat shim — upgrade
18	// client and server together.
19	const SpecCurrentVersion = 1
20	
21	// Mode controls how the work terminates.
22	type Mode string
23	
24	const (
25		// ModeOnce runs one bead (or none if the queue is empty) then exits.
26		ModeOnce Mode = "once"
27		// ModeDrain processes all ready beads then exits.
28		ModeDrain Mode = "drain"
29		// ModeWatch processes all ready beads, then sleeps for IdleInterval and
30		// rechecks, looping indefinitely until interrupted.
31		ModeWatch Mode = "watch"
32	)
33	
34	// Duration wraps time.Duration with JSON support that marshals as a string
35	// (e.g., "30s") and unmarshals both strings ("30s") and numeric nanoseconds.
36	type Duration struct {
37		time.Duration
38	}
39	
40	func (d Duration) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
41		return json.Marshal(d.String())
42	}
43	
44	func (d *Duration) UnmarshalJSON(b []byte) error {
45		// Try string first.
46		var s string
47		if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &s); err == nil {
48			parsed, err := time.ParseDuration(s)
49			if err != nil {
50				return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: invalid duration string %q: %w", s, err)
51			}
52			d.Duration = parsed
53			return nil
54		}
55		// Try numeric nanoseconds.
56		var ns int64
57		if err := json.Unmarshal(b, &ns); err == nil {
58			d.Duration = time.Duration(ns)
59			return nil
60		}
61		return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: cannot unmarshal %s as duration (want string or nanosecond integer)", strconv.Quote(string(b)))
62	}
63	
64	// ExecuteLoopSpec is the canonical, transport-agnostic specification for an
65	// work worker. All transports (cobra, REST, GraphQL) map into this type
66	// before persisting or dispatching.
67	type ExecuteLoopSpec struct {
68		ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root,omitempty"`
69		Harness     string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
70		Model       string `json:"model,omitempty"`
71		Profile     string `json:"profile,omitempty"`
72		Provider    string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
73		Effort      string `json:"effort,omitempty"`
74		LabelFilter string `json:"label_filter,omitempty"`
75		// AttemptBackend selects the execute-bead workspace/transport backend for
76		// each attempt. Empty uses executions.attempt_backend or the binary default.
77		AttemptBackend string `json:"attempt_backend,omitempty"`
78	
79		// Mode controls loop termination. Defaults to ModeDrain via ApplyDefaults.
80		Mode Mode `json:"mode,omitempty"`
81	
82		// IdleInterval is the sleep duration between queue polls when Mode=watch
83		// and no execution-ready work is found.
84		IdleInterval Duration `json:"idle_interval,omitempty"`
85	
86		NoReview               bool   `json:"no_review,omitempty"`
87		ReviewHarness          string `json:"review_harness,omitempty"`
88		ReviewModel            string `json:"review_model,omitempty"`
89		IgnoreCooldown         bool   `json:"ignore_cooldown,omitempty"`
90		CooldownOverrideReason string `json:"cooldown_override_reason,omitempty"`
91	
92		// OpaquePassthrough skips DDx-side route validation and config
93		// harness/model injection (CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-010).
94		OpaquePassthrough bool `json:"opaque_passthrough,omitempty"`
95	
96		MaxCostUSD         float64  `json:"max_cost_usd,omitempty"`
97		MaxBeadCostUSD     float64  `json:"max_bead_cost_usd,omitempty"`
98		MaxRecoveryCostUSD float64  `json:"max_recovery_cost_usd,omitempty"`
99		PreClaimTimeout    Duration `json:"preclaim_timeout,omitempty"`
100		RequestTimeout     Duration `json:"request_timeout,omitempty"`
101		RateLimitMaxWait   Duration `json:"rate_limit_max_wait,omitempty"`
102		MinPower           int      `json:"min_power,omitempty"`
103		MaxPower           int      `json:"max_power,omitempty"`
104	
105		// FromRev, if set, narrows execution to beads introduced after this git revision.
106		FromRev string `json:"from_rev,omitempty"`
107	
108		// SpecVersion must equal SpecCurrentVersion. See SpecCurrentVersion for the
109		// client/server forward-compat stance.
110		SpecVersion int `json:"spec_version,omitempty"`
111	}
112	
113	// DispatchOptions carries control-plane fields used at dispatch time. It is not
114	// persisted into the worker record.
115	type DispatchOptions struct {
116		// Local, when true, runs the worker in-process rather than via the server API.
117		Local bool `json:"local,omitempty"`
118		// JSON is the raw JSON payload to forward when dispatching via REST/GraphQL.
119		// Callers that build the request body manually may set this instead of
120		// populating an ExecuteLoopSpec.
121		JSON string `json:"json,omitempty"`
122	}
123	
124	// ApplyDefaults fills in zero-value fields with their canonical defaults.
125	// Safe to call on a zero-value spec.
126	func (s *ExecuteLoopSpec) ApplyDefaults() {
127		if s.Mode == "" {
128			s.Mode = ModeDrain
129		}
130		if s.Mode == ModeWatch && s.IdleInterval.Duration == 0 {
131			s.IdleInterval = Duration{30 * time.Second}
132		}
133		if s.SpecVersion == 0 {
134			s.SpecVersion = SpecCurrentVersion
135		}
136		if s.MaxRecoveryCostUSD == 0 {
137			s.MaxRecoveryCostUSD = escalation.DefaultMaxRecoveryCostUSD
138		}
139	}
140	
141	// Validate checks that the spec is well-formed after ApplyDefaults has run.
142	func (s *ExecuteLoopSpec) Validate() error {
143		switch s.Mode {
144		case ModeOnce, ModeDrain, ModeWatch:
145		default:
146			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: unknown mode %q (want once, drain, or watch)", s.Mode)
147		}
148		if s.Mode != ModeWatch && s.IdleInterval.Duration != 0 {
149			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: idle_interval is only valid when mode=watch")
150		}
151		if s.SpecVersion != 0 && s.SpecVersion != SpecCurrentVersion {
152			return fmt.Errorf("executeloop: unsupported spec_version %d (want %d)", s.SpecVersion, SpecCurrentVersion)
153		}
154		return nil
155	}

38	}

40	// emitPhase centralises the phase-state transition contract so callers only
41	// need to name the phase. Terminal transitions emit both the terminal phase
42	// record and the durable bead.result record.
43	func emitPhase(ctx context.Context, store PhaseStore, beadID string, phase Phase, outcome Outcome) error {
44		if store == nil {
45			return nil
46		}
47		switch phase {
48		case PhaseQueueing, PhaseRunning:
49			return store.EmitProgress(ctx, beadID, phase, outcome)
50		case PhaseTerminal:
51			if err := store.EmitProgress(ctx, beadID, phase, outcome); err != nil {
52				return err
53			}
54			return store.EmitResult(ctx, beadID, outcome)
55		default:
56			return fmt.Errorf("unknown phase %q", phase)
57		}
58	}

60	// EmitPhase is the exported wrapper used by the agent package.
61	func EmitPhase(ctx context.Context, store PhaseStore, beadID string, phase Phase, outcome Outcome) error {
62		return emitPhase(ctx, store, beadID, phase, outcome)
63	}

127	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
128		mode := r.Mode
129		if mode == "" {
130			if r.Once {
131				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
132			} else {
133				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
134			}
135		}
136		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
137		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
138			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
139		}
140		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
141			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
142		}
143		return mode, idleInterval
144	}

297			if berr != nil {
298				if runtime.Log != nil {
299					_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition park error: %v\n", berr)
300				}
301			}
302		}
303	
304		// Queue-level depth check: orchestrator uses its own cap, not the
305		// implementation prompt's hardcoded depth-2 cap.
306		maxDepth := rcfg.MaxDecompositionDepth()
307		if maxDepth > 0 && storeBeadDepth(ctx, w.Store, candidate) >= maxDepth {
308			overflowBody, _ := json.Marshal(map[string]any{
309				"depth": storeBeadDepth(ctx, w.Store, candidate),
310				"max":   maxDepth,
311			})
312			_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
313				Kind:      "triage-overflow",
314				Summary:   "depth cap exceeded during post-attempt decomposition",
315				Body:      string(overflowBody),
316				Actor:     assignee,
317				Source:    "ddx work",
318				CreatedAt: at,
319			})
320			parkOperator("queue-level depth cap exceeded; operator must split")
321			return
322		}
323	
324		decomp, err := runtime.PostAttemptDecompositionHook(ctx, candidate.ID)
325		if err != nil {
326			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition hook error: %s", err.Error()))
327			return
328		}
329		if decomp == nil {
330			parkOperator("decomposition hook returned no split")
331			return
332		}
333		lossyOrEmpty := isDecompositionLossy(decomp.ACMap) || (len(decomp.ACMap) == 0 && strings.TrimSpace(candidate.Acceptance) != "")
334		if lossyOrEmpty {
335			parkOperator("decomposition AC map is incomplete; operator must produce a lossless split")
336			return
337		}
338	
339		childIDs, decompErr := applyPreClaimDecomposition(ctx, w.Store, candidate, decomp, assignee, at)
340		if decompErr != nil {
341			parkOperator(fmt.Sprintf("decomposition apply error: %s", decompErr.Error()))
342			return
343		}
344		if runtime.Log != nil {
345			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt decomposition: bead %s split into %s\n", candidate.ID, strings.Join(childIDs, ", "))
346		}
347		emit("post_attempt_decomposition.applied", map[string]any{
348			"bead_id":   candidate.ID,
349			"child_ids": childIDs,
350		})
351	}

353	// runPostAttemptTriage invokes the optional post-attempt triage hook after
354	// the attempt outcome has been finalized but before any cooldown decision is
355	// applied. Hook failures are fail-open: the report is returned unchanged and
356	// legacy cooldown behavior remains in force.
357	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) runPostAttemptTriage(ctx context.Context, candidate bead.Bead, report ExecuteBeadReport, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, assignee string, now func() time.Time) ExecuteBeadReport {
358		hook := runtime.PostAttemptTriageHook
359		if hook == nil {
360			return report
361		}
362		if isDeterministicSystemOutcomeReason(report.OutcomeReason) {
363			return report
364		}
365		triage, err := hook(ctx, candidate.ID, report)
366		if err != nil {
367			if runtime.Log != nil {
368				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, err)
369			}
370			return report
371		}
372		triage = normalizeTriageResult(triage)
373		hasWarnings := len(triage.DecodeWarnings) > 0 || triage.Malformed
374		classificationRecognized := isRecognizedTriageClassification(triage.Classification)
375		if hasWarnings && runtime.Log != nil {
376			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "post-attempt triage warning (%s %s): %s (continuing)\n", candidate.ID, report.Status, formatTriageWarnings(triage.DecodeWarnings))
377		}
378		if !classificationRecognized && !hasWarnings {
379			return report
380		}
381		if classificationRecognized {
382			report.OutcomeReason = triage.Classification
383		}
384		recordPostAttemptTriageEvent(w.Store, candidate.ID, report, triage, assignee, now().UTC())
385		return report
386	}

400	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
401	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
402	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
403	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
404		if d <= 0 {
405			return 0
406		}
407		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
408			return MaxLoopCooldown
409		}
410		return d
411	}

feat: add helix-system bead kinds and queue injection API [ddx-fb841e48]

Add two new system-generated bead kinds (review-finding, alignment-review) to
support structured review and alignment findings as first-class tracker beads.

- Add IssueTypeReviewFinding and IssueTypeAlignmentReview constants to types.go
- Implement Store.Inject() method for idempotent bead creation with parent refs
- Implement ReviewFindingPayload and AlignmentReviewPayload with validation
- Add execute-loop dispatchers (runReviewFinding, runAlignmentReview) that
  record findings as bead events with standard evidence-bundle structure
- Add comprehensive tests for kind registration, idempotent injection, and
  payload extraction/validation

Acceptance Criteria:
- AC1: Kinds register, validate parent refs, reject invalid payloads ✓
- AC2: Inject creates ready beads, idempotent on (parent, kind, payload-hash) ✓
- AC3: Execute-loop dispatchers handle new kinds, write standard evidence bundles ✓
- AC4: All tests pass (green), lefthook passes, go vet clean ✓

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.text:
The bead execution is complete. All acceptance criteria have been satisfied:

✅ **AC1**: Kinds register and validate — `ReviewFindingPayload` and `AlignmentReviewPayload` implement `InjectPayload` with `Kind()` and `Validate()` methods.

✅ **AC2**: `Inject()` creates idempotent beads — payload hash ensures duplicate payloads return the same bead ID; different payloads create new beads.

25	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
26	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
27	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
28		parent := os.Environ()
29		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
30		for _, kv := range parent {
31			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
32				continue
33			}
34			env = append(env, kv)
35		}
36		return env
37	}
38	
39	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
40	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
41	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
42		t.Helper()
43		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
44		cmd.Dir = dir
45		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
46		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
47		if err != nil {
48			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
49		}
50		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
